name: Glassmorphism
summary: A layered surface style using translucent panels, blur, light borders, and controlled depth.

palette: cool neutrals, soft accents, and background color fields
typography: clean sans-serif with high contrast on panels
surfaces: frosted panels, translucent cards, and blurred backdrops
effects: backdrop blur, light border, soft shadow
layout: layered depth with clear foreground content
imagery: soft gradient backdrops and app UI surfaces

recommendations: Use sleek, modern, calm visual tone.
avoid: Avoid implying fragility for heavy workflows.

recommendations: Define backdrop, glass surface, border, and on-glass text roles.
avoid: Avoid low-contrast text over transparent surfaces.
Avoid thin body weights on glass.

recommendations: Use light outline icons with sufficient contrast.
avoid: Avoid faint icons on blurred backdrops.

recommendations: Use solid primary buttons on glass surfaces.
Use blur, border, and shadow as named surface tokens.
avoid: Avoid stacked blur on every card.

recommendations: Use translucent nav only when text contrast is stable.
Use frosted panel with opaque content areas.
Use one glass layer over a calm backdrop.

avoid: Avoid blur animation as primary motion.

recommendations: Increase border clarity or lift slightly.
Use quick overlay fades and depth shifts.
Use distinct backdrop, panel, and foreground layers.
Use solid status text and icons on glass.

accessibilityRisks.risk: Transparent surfaces can fail contrast depending on backdrop.
accessibilityRisks.mitigation: Require contrast-tested text surfaces and opaque fallbacks.

css: Use background rgba, backdrop-filter, border, and shadow tokens.
avoid: Do not place critical text on uncontrolled imagery.
